nodes.title: Activation Collection
widgets_values:
@torch.no_grad()
def collect_layer_activations(
    tokenizer,
    model,
    texts: List[str],
    max_length: int = 128,
    batch_size: int = 4,
    max_samples_per_layer: int = 12000,
    remove_special_tokens: bool = True,
) -> Dict[int, np.ndarray]:
    """
    Returns:
        activations_by_layer[layer_idx] = np.ndarray of shape [N, hidden_dim]
    """
    activations_by_layer: Dict[int, List[np.ndarray]] = {}

    for batch_texts in batched(texts, batch_size):
        encoded = tokenizer(
            batch_texts,
            return_tensors="pt",
            padding=True,
            truncation=True,
            max_length=max_length,
        )
        input_ids = encoded["input_ids"].to(DEVICE)
        attention_mask = encoded["attention_mask"].to(DEVICE)

        outputs = model(
            input_ids=input_ids,
            attention_mask=attention_mask,
            output_hidden_states=True,
            use_cache=False,
        )

        hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states
        valid_mask = attention_mask.bool()

        if remove_special_tokens:
            special_ids = set(tokenizer.all_special_ids or [])
            if special_ids:
                non_special = torch.ones_like(valid_mask, dtype=torch.bool)
                for sid in special_ids:
                    non_special &= (input_ids != sid)
                valid_mask &= non_special

        for layer_idx, h in enumerate(hidden_states):
            h = h.detach().float().cpu()
            mask = valid_mask.detach().cpu()
            flat = h[mask]
            if flat.numel() == 0:
                continue

            flat_np = flat.numpy()
            if layer_idx not in activations_by_layer:
                activations_by_layer[layer_idx] = []
            activations_by_layer[layer_idx].append(flat_np)

    out: Dict[int, np.ndarray] = {}
    rng = np.random.default_rng(SEED)

    for layer_idx, chunks in activations_by_layer.items():
        x = np.concatenate(chunks, axis=0)
        if x.shape[0] > max_samples_per_layer:
            idx = rng.choice(x.shape[0], size=max_samples_per_layer, replace=False)
            x = x[idx]
        out[layer_idx] = x

    return out


print("Collecting activations...")
activations_by_layer = collect_layer_activations(
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    model=model,
    texts=chunk_texts,
    max_length=MAX_LENGTH,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    max_samples_per_layer=MAX_SAMPLES_PER_LAYER,
    remove_special_tokens=REMOVE_SPECIAL_TOKENS,
)

for layer_idx in sorted(activations_by_layer.keys()):
    x = activations_by_layer[layer_idx]
    print(f"Layer {layer_idx:>2}: activations shape = {x.shape}")

nodes.title: PCA Analysis
widgets_values:
def run_pca_per_layer(
    activations_by_layer: Dict[int, np.ndarray],
    n_components: int = 128,
) -> List[LayerPCAResult]:
    results: List[LayerPCAResult] = []

    for layer_idx in sorted(activations_by_layer.keys()):
        x = activations_by_layer[layer_idx]
        n_samples, hidden_dim = x.shape

        k = min(n_samples, hidden_dim) if n_components is None else min(n_components, n_samples, hidden_dim)
        if k < 2:
            continue

        if n_components is None:
            pca = PCA(n_components=None, svd_solver="full")
        else:
            pca = PCA(n_components=k, svd_solver="randomized", random_state=SEED)
        pca.fit(x)

        results.append(
            LayerPCAResult(
                layer_idx=layer_idx,
                explained_variance_ratio=pca.explained_variance_ratio_,
                explained_variance=pca.explained_variance_,
                singular_values=pca.singular_values_,
                n_samples=n_samples,
                hidden_dim=hidden_dim,
            )
        )

    return results


def summarize_intrinsic_dimension(
    results: List[LayerPCAResult],
    thresholds: Tuple[float, ...] = (0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99),
):
    print("\n=== Layer-wise intrinsic dimension summary ===")
    header = ["layer", "samples", "hidden_dim"] + [f"PCs@{int(t*100)}%" for t in thresholds]
    print(" | ".join(f"{h:>10}" for h in header))
    print("-" * (13 * len(header)))

    for r in results:
        cum = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio)
        vals = []
        for t in thresholds:
            idx = int(np.searchsorted(cum, t, side="left")) + 1
            vals.append(idx)
        row = [r.layer_idx, r.n_samples, r.hidden_dim] + vals
        print(" | ".join(f"{str(v):>10}" for v in row))


print("Running PCA per layer...")
results = run_pca_per_layer(
    activations_by_layer=activations_by_layer,
    n_components=N_COMPONENTS,
)

summarize_intrinsic_dimension(results)
print(f"Computed PCA for {len(results)} layers.")
ComfyUI

nodes.title: Plots + Quick Summary
widgets_values:
def plot_eigenvalue_decay(results: List[LayerPCAResult], save_prefix: str = "activation_pca"):
    if not results:
        raise ValueError("No PCA results to plot.")

    candidate_indices = [0, len(results) // 4, len(results) // 2, len(results) - 1]
    candidate_indices = sorted(set(i for i in candidate_indices if 0 <= i < len(results)))
    selected = [results[i] for i in candidate_indices]

    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        x = np.arange(1, len(r.explained_variance_ratio) + 1)
        plt.plot(x, r.explained_variance_ratio, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Explained variance ratio")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: explained variance ratio decay")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_evr_decay.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        cum = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio)
        x = np.arange(1, len(cum) + 1)
        plt.plot(x, cum, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Cumulative explained variance")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: cumulative explained variance")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_cumulative.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        x = np.arange(1, len(r.explained_variance) + 1)
        plt.semilogy(x, r.explained_variance, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Eigenvalue / variance (log scale)")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: eigenvalue decay")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_eigenvalues_log.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    return selected


def print_quick_takeaways(results: List[LayerPCAResult]):
    print("\n=== Quick takeaways ===")
    if not results:
        print("No results available.")
        return

    for r in [results[0], results[len(results)//2], results[-1]]:
        cum = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio)
        pcs_90 = int(np.searchsorted(cum, 0.90, side="left")) + 1
        pcs_95 = int(np.searchsorted(cum, 0.95, side="left")) + 1
        print(
            f"Layer {r.layer_idx}: {pcs_90} PCs for 90% variance, "
            f"{pcs_95} PCs for 95% variance (hidden dim = {r.hidden_dim})."
        )


selected_layers = plot_eigenvalue_decay(results, save_prefix=SAVE_PREFIX)
print_quick_takeaways(results)
print("Saved plot files:")
print(f"- {SAVE_PREFIX}_evr_decay.png")
print(f"- {SAVE_PREFIX}_cumulative.png")
print(f"- {SAVE_PREFIX}_eigenvalues_log.png")

​
=== Quick takeaways ===
Layer 0: 269 PCs for 90% variance, 404 PCs for 95% variance (hidden dim = 1024).
Layer 12: 341 PCs for 90% variance, 499 PCs for 95% variance (hidden dim = 1024).
Layer 24: 326 PCs for 90% variance, 494 PCs for 95% variance (hidden dim = 1024).

- qwen35_08b_activation_pca_evr_decay.png

def plot_eigenvalue_decay(results: List[LayerPCAResult], save_prefix: str = "activation_pca"):
    if not results:
        raise ValueError("No PCA results to plot.")

    candidate_indices = [0, len(results) // 4, len(results) // 2, len(results) - 1]
    candidate_indices = sorted(set(i for i in candidate_indices if 0 <= i < len(results)))
    selected = [results[i] for i in candidate_indices]

    # 1) Explained variance ratio decay
    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        x = np.arange(1, len(r.explained_variance_ratio) + 1)
        plt.plot(x, r.explained_variance_ratio, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Explained variance ratio")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: explained variance ratio decay")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_evr_decay.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    # 2) Cumulative explained variance
    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        cum = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio)
        x = np.arange(1, len(cum) + 1)
        plt.plot(x, cum, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Cumulative explained variance")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: cumulative explained variance")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_cumulative.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    # 3) Per-component variance percentage on log scale
    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
    for r in selected:
        x = np.arange(1, len(r.explained_variance_ratio) + 1)
        pct = 100 * r.explained_variance_ratio
        plt.semilogy(x, pct, label=f"layer {r.layer_idx}")
    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Explained variance (%) [log scale]")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: variance percentage decay")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"{save_prefix}_variance_percentage_log.png", dpi=160)
    plt.show()

    return selected


def print_quick_takeaways(results: List[LayerPCAResult]):
    print("\n=== Quick takeaways ===")
    if not results:
        print("No results available.")
        return

    for r in [results[0], results[len(results)//2], results[-1]]:
        cum = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio)
        pcs_90 = int(np.searchsorted(cum, 0.90, side="left")) + 1
        pcs_95 = int(np.searchsorted(cum, 0.95, side="left")) + 1
        print(
            f"Layer {r.layer_idx}: {pcs_90} PCs for 90% variance, "
            f"{pcs_95} PCs for 95% variance (hidden dim = {r.hidden_dim})."
        )


selected_layers = plot_eigenvalue_decay(results, save_prefix=SAVE_PREFIX)

def plot_cumulative_all_layers(results, save_prefix="activation_pca"):
    if not results:
        raise ValueError("No PCA results to plot.")

    # choose a good perceptual colormap
    cmap = plt.get_cmap("magma")  # or "plasma", "magma"

    n = len(results)

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 6))
    
    STEP = 1  # change to 3, 4, etc.

    for i, r in enumerate(results):
        if i % STEP != 0:
            continue

        cum_pct = np.cumsum(r.explained_variance_ratio) * 100
        x = np.arange(1, len(cum_pct) + 1)

        color = cmap(i / (len(results) - 1))
        plt.plot(x, cum_pct, color=color, linewidth=2, alpha=0.9)

    # colorbar to show mapping
    sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap=cmap)
    sm.set_array([0, n - 1])
    cbar = fig.colorbar(sm, ax=ax)
    cbar.set_label("Layer index")

    plt.xlabel("Principal component")
    plt.ylabel("Cumulative explained variance (%)")
    plt.title("Activation PCA: cumulative variance (all layers)")
    plt.ylim(0, 110)
    plt.xscale('log')

    # optional reference lines
    plt.axhline(100, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
    # plt.axhline(95, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

selected_layers = plot_cumulative_all_layers(results)

#### Cell: Imports + Config ####
"""
Activation geometry workflow, split into multiple notebook cells.

Cell 1: imports and configuration.
Run cells from top to bottom.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import random
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Optional

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import requests
import torch
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer


# ----------------------------
# Configuration
# ----------------------------

MODEL_NAME = "Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B"
DEVICE = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
DTYPE = torch.float16 if torch.cuda.is_available() else torch.float32

MAX_LENGTH = 128
BATCH_SIZE = 4
MAX_SAMPLES_PER_LAYER = 12000
N_COMPONENTS = None
REMOVE_SPECIAL_TOKENS = True
SEED = 42
SAVE_PREFIX = "qwen35_08b_activation_pca"

# Text source / chunking
GUTENBERG_URL = "https://www.gutenberg.org/files/11/11-0.txt"
TOKEN_START = 500
TOKEN_LENGTH = 6000      # increase if you want more tokens
CHUNK_SIZE = MAX_LENGTH   # keep aligned with model input length
STRIDE = MAX_LENGTH       # use 64 for overlap if you want denser sampling

random.seed(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)
torch.manual_seed(SEED)

print(f"Configured model: {MODEL_NAME}")
print(f"Device: {DEVICE}, dtype: {DTYPE}")
print(f"Gutenberg source: {GUTENBERG_URL}")

nodes.title: Model Loading

#### Cell: Model Loading + Text Preparation ####
def load_model_and_tokenizer(model_name: str):
    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, trust_remote_code=True)

    if tokenizer.pad_token is None and tokenizer.eos_token is not None:
        tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token

    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        model_name,
        torch_dtype=DTYPE,
        trust_remote_code=True,
        low_cpu_mem_usage=True,
    )
    model.to(DEVICE)
    model.eval()
    return tokenizer, model


def build_chunk_texts_from_gutenberg(
    tokenizer,
    url: str,
    token_start: int = 500,
    token_length: int = 6000,
    chunk_size: int = 128,
    stride: int = 128,
) -> List[str]:
    response = requests.get(url, timeout=30)
    response.raise_for_status()

    full_text = response.text
    clean_text = clean_gutenberg(full_text)

    tokens = tokenizer(clean_text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=False)
    input_ids = tokens["input_ids"][0]

    chunk_ids = input_ids[token_start : token_start + token_length]

    chunk_texts: List[str] = []
    for i in range(0, len(chunk_ids) - chunk_size + 1, stride):
        chunk = chunk_ids[i : i + chunk_size]
        text_chunk = tokenizer.decode(chunk, clean_up_tokenization_spaces=False)
        chunk_texts.append(text_chunk)

    return chunk_texts


print(f"Loading model: {MODEL_NAME}")
tokenizer, model = load_model_and_tokenizer(MODEL_NAME)
print("Model and tokenizer loaded.")

chunk_texts = build_chunk_texts_from_gutenberg(
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    url=GUTENBERG_URL,
    token_start=TOKEN_START,
    token_length=TOKEN_LENGTH,
    chunk_size=CHUNK_SIZE,
    stride=STRIDE,
)

print(f"Created {len(chunk_texts)} text chunks.")
if chunk_texts:
    print("First chunk preview:")
    print(chunk_texts[0][:500])

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import make_swiss_roll, make_s_curve
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA

for seed in range(3):
    # ----------------------------
    # Generate swiss roll in 3D
    # ----------------------------
    X, t = make_swiss_roll(n_samples=3000, noise=0.0, random_state=seed)
    # X, t = make_s_curve(n_samples=3000, noise=0.05, random_state=42)

    def random_rotation_matrix(seed=seed):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)

        # random matrix
        A = rng.normal(size=(3, 3))

        # QR decomposition → orthonormal basis
        Q, R = np.linalg.qr(A)

        # ensure right-handed system (det = +1)
        if np.linalg.det(Q) < 0:
            Q[:, 0] *= -1

        return Q


    # apply rotation
    R = random_rotation_matrix()
    X = X @ R

    # stretch/compress selected axes
    scale_factors = np.array([18, 0.7, 1.3])   # x, y, z scaling in rotated coordinates
    S = np.diag(scale_factors)
    X = X @ S

    # ----------------------------
    # PCA: keep all 3 PCs
    # ----------------------------
    pca3 = PCA(n_components=3)
    X_pca3 = pca3.fit_transform(X)

    # ----------------------------
    # PCA: keep only 2 PCs
    # ----------------------------
    pca2 = PCA(n_components=2)
    X_pca2 = pca2.fit_transform(X)

    # ----------------------------
    # Check distance preservation
    # ----------------------------
    idx1, idx2 = 0, 100
    d_orig = np.linalg.norm(X[idx1] - X[idx2])
    d_pca3 = np.linalg.norm(X_pca3[idx1] - X_pca3[idx2])

    # print(f"Distance in original 3D space: {d_orig:.6f}")
    # print(f"Distance after PCA with 3 PCs: {d_pca3:.6f}")
    # print(f"Difference: {abs(d_orig - d_pca3):.6e}")

    # ----------------------------
    # Plot
    # ----------------------------
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(15, 4))

    # Original swiss roll
    ax1 = fig.add_subplot(131, projection="3d")
    sc1 = ax1.scatter(X[:, 0], X[:, 1], X[:, 2], c=t, s=5)
    ax1.set_title("Original swiss roll")
    ax1.set_xlabel("x")
    ax1.set_ylabel("y")
    ax1.set_zlabel("z")
    set_equal_3d(ax1, X)

    # PCA with all 3 PCs
    ax2 = fig.add_subplot(132, projection="3d")
    sc2 = ax2.scatter(X_pca3[:, 0], X_pca3[:, 1], X_pca3[:, 2], c=t, s=5)
    ax2.set_title("PCA with all 3 PCs")
    ax2.set_xlabel("PC1")
    ax2.set_ylabel("PC2")
    ax2.set_zlabel("PC3")
    set_equal_3d(ax2, X_pca3)

    # PCA with only 2 PCs
    ax3 = fig.add_subplot(133)
    sc3 = ax3.scatter(X_pca2[:, 0], X_pca2[:, 1], c=t, s=5)
    ax3.set_title("PCA with only 2 PCs")
    ax3.set_xlabel("PC1")
    ax3.set_ylabel("PC2")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    Notebook.add_plot()
    
    print("\n=== PCA (3 components) ===")
    print("Eigenvalues:", pca3.explained_variance_)
    print("Explained variance ratio:", pca3.explained_variance_ratio_)
    print("Sum ratio:", pca3.explained_variance_ratio_.sum())

def set_equal_3d(ax, X):
    x = X[:, 0]
    y = X[:, 1]
    z = X[:, 2]

    x_mid = 0.5 * (x.max() + x.min())
    y_mid = 0.5 * (y.max() + y.min())
    z_mid = 0.5 * (z.max() + z.min())

    max_range = max(
        x.max() - x.min(),
        y.max() - y.min(),
        z.max() - z.min(),
    ) / 2.0

    ax.set_xlim(x_mid - max_range, x_mid + max_range)
    ax.set_ylim(y_mid - max_range, y_mid + max_range)
    ax.set_zlim(z_mid - max_range, z_mid + max_range)
    ax.set_box_aspect([1, 1, 1])

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import make_swiss_roll


def random_rotation_matrix(seed=42):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    A = rng.normal(size=(3, 3))
    Q, _ = np.linalg.qr(A)
    if np.linalg.det(Q) < 0:
        Q[:, 0] *= -1
    return Q


def orthonormal_basis_from_normal(n):
    """Given a normal vector n, return two orthonormal vectors spanning the projection plane."""
    n = np.asarray(n, dtype=float)
    n = n / np.linalg.norm(n)

    if abs(n[0]) < 0.9:
        a = np.array([1.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    else:
        a = np.array([0.0, 1.0, 0.0])

    u1 = a - np.dot(a, n) * n
    u1 = u1 / np.linalg.norm(u1)
    u2 = np.cross(n, u1)
    u2 = u2 / np.linalg.norm(u2)

    return u1, u2


def generate_smooth_random_directions(
    n_frames,
    seed=0,
    accel_scale=0.08,
    damping=0.92,
    init_dir=None,
    init_vel=None,
):
    """
    Generate a smooth random trajectory of unit directions on the sphere.

    accel_scale: larger -> more wandering / diversity
    damping: closer to 1 -> smoother, more inertial motion
    """
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)

    if init_dir is None:
        d = rng.normal(size=3)
        d /= np.linalg.norm(d)
    else:
        d = np.asarray(init_dir, dtype=float)
        d /= np.linalg.norm(d)

    if init_vel is None:
        v = np.zeros(3, dtype=float)
    else:
        v = np.asarray(init_vel, dtype=float)

    directions = []

    for _ in range(n_frames):
        # random acceleration
        a = rng.normal(size=3)
        a *= accel_scale

        # update velocity with damping
        v = damping * v + a

        # keep motion tangent to sphere so direction changes smoothly
        v = v - np.dot(v, d) * d

        # update direction
        d = d + v
        d /= np.linalg.norm(d)

        # re-project velocity to tangent plane after normalization
        v = v - np.dot(v, d) * d

        directions.append(d.copy())

    return directions


# ----------------------------
# 1) Generate swiss roll
# ----------------------------
X, t = make_swiss_roll(n_samples=3000, noise=0.8, random_state=42)

# Optional: rotate once so it is not axis-aligned
R = random_rotation_matrix(seed=7)
X = X @ R

# Optional: stretch/compress some directions
S = np.diag([1.8, 0.8, 1.2])
X = X @ S


# ----------------------------
# 2) Generate smooth random viewing directions
# ----------------------------
n_frames = 500
directions = generate_smooth_random_directions(
    n_frames=n_frames,
    seed=123,
    accel_scale=0.1,   # try 0.03 for smoother, 0.12 for more diversity
    damping=0.99,       # try 0.98 for slower drift, 0.85 for more jitter
)

projection_frames = []

for n in directions:
    u1, u2 = orthonormal_basis_from_normal(n)
    X_proj = X @ np.stack([u1, u2], axis=1)
    projection_frames.append(X_proj)


# ----------------------------
# 3) Compute global bounds so every frame uses same scale
# ----------------------------
all_proj = np.vstack(projection_frames)

xmin, xmax = all_proj[:, 0].min(), all_proj[:, 0].max()
ymin, ymax = all_proj[:, 1].min(), all_proj[:, 1].max()

max_range = max(xmax - xmin, ymax - ymin) / 2.0
xmid = 0.5 * (xmin + xmax)
ymid = 0.5 * (ymin + ymax)


# ----------------------------
# 4) Plot one 2D projection per frame
# ----------------------------
for k, (n, X_proj) in enumerate(zip(directions, projection_frames)):
    plt.figure(figsize=(5, 5))
    plt.scatter(X_proj[:, 0], X_proj[:, 1], c=t, s=5)

    plt.xlim(xmid - max_range, xmid + max_range)
    plt.ylim(ymid - max_range, ymid + max_range)
    plt.gca().set_aspect("equal", adjustable="box")

    plt.title(
        f"2D projection view {k+1}/{n_frames}\n"
        f"normal = [{n[0]:.2f}, {n[1]:.2f}, {n[2]:.2f}]"
    )
    plt.xlabel("proj dim 1")
    plt.ylabel("proj dim 2")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    print(k, end=" ")
    Notebook.add_plot()
